findings.problem: The narrative pre-confirms Minjae's second call before Ina investigates, so the bridge confrontation delivers confirmation rather than revelation. This blunts suspense and weakens the chapter's central turn from trust to structural betrayal.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the end of the workshop scene at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:141 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:153 to remove declarative confirmation of who Minjae calls. Keep suspicion, but preserve uncertainty until Ina's evidence-gathering and confrontation at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:269 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:339 so the admission changes what both reader and focalizer know.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:339, the text gives no factual narrator statement that Minjae called Kwon; betrayal must first become certain through Ina's evidence process or Minjae's spoken admission. Fail if the narrator states the hidden call as fact earlier.

findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the intimacy seam: the scene introduces potentially coercive pressure (wrist pinning, scar pain, finger marks), then self-soothes with a single interpretive line and pivots to logistics. The prose looks away from embodied emotional consequence right where the trust fracture should deepen.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:109, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:121 so the aftermath explicitly stages the consent/trust ambiguity instead of bypassing it. Keep physical specificity, but add an immediate beat where Ina names what the pressure meant to her and Minjae responds in-scene before operational planning resumes.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:107 to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:123 includes one explicit interior or spoken boundary statement from Ina and one direct response from Minjae that alters trust perception before route-planning dialogue resumes. Fail if the scene moves to logistics without that reckoning.

findings.problem: Timidity at the chapter exit: the abduction beat cuts off at door unlock/driver neutrality, withholding the first embodied contact. Given established coercive stakes, this reads as a cinematic retreat from immediate physical reality.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand the ending at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:507 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:509 by one short beat of concrete contact (command, grip, movement into van, sensory detail) while still preserving the handoff into chapter 14.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised ending shows at least one physical interaction between Ina and the van team plus one immediate thought or tactical decision from Ina before the chapter break. Fail if the scene still cuts before first contact.

findings.problem: Bridge confrontation dialogue drifts into long thesis turns and shared rhetorical cadence. Ina and Minjae become too interchangeable at peak pressure, weakening idiolect separation required by the style bible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the confrontation centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:371, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:413, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:447 into shorter contested turns. Push Minjae toward clipped, operational, self-interrupting syntax and push Ina toward moral-media framing followed by blunt corrective bursts so each turn sounds speaker-specific under stress.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised span from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:371 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:447, neither speaker holds the floor for more than three consecutive sentences and each speaker has at least two turns with distinct register markers (Minjae: practical interruption/logistics slang; Ina: framing language then hard plain correction). Fail if long interchangeable thesis speeches remain.

findings.problem: Mechanical lexical reuse flattens the confrontation: repeated route/lane/leverage/board jargon makes the exchange feel token-driven rather than emotionally adaptive. This reads like over-literal reuse of style-bible stress vocabulary.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:345, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:417, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:471, reduce repeated operational jargon loops and substitute concrete personal stakes (sister transfer conditions, eviction paper, hospital billing pressure, wrist-mark aftermath) so diction varies with emotional escalation.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised confrontation uses route/lane/leverage/board terminology no more than twice total in this span and adds at least three concrete non-jargon stakes tied to body, money, or family. Fail if jargon remains the dominant diction across multiple consecutive turns.

findings.problem: Recurring beat formulas appear more than twice: humorless-laugh markers and loud-transit interruption beats are reused as default tension punctuation. The repetition makes late-scene rhythm feel pre-patterned instead of freshly observed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:325, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:409, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:449, plus adjacent setup beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:59, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:343, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:425, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:487, to diversify reaction mechanics. Replace repeated laugh/noise templates with varied physical action, silence, or interior micro-shifts tied to leverage changes.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the chapter contains at most two humorless-laugh beats and at most two external-noise interruption beats, with remaining tension transitions carried by distinct mechanisms visible on the page. Fail if both templates still recur three or more times.

summary: Chapter 13 lands major spine events and maintains canonical past tense and continuity facts, but it does not clear pass-level quality. The betrayal engine is partially spoiled early, the intimacy-to-betrayal seam sanitizes consequence, confrontation dialogue collapses idiolect distinction into thesis rhetoric with jargon loops, and repeated prose templates flatten scene rhythm.
